title: True, False, and remainders
body: Some values are simply *True* or *False* — those are called *booleans*, and they're how code makes decisions. You get one by comparing things: `==` asks "are these equal?". There's also `%`, which gives the *remainder* after dividing — `10 % 2` is `0`, so `n % 2 == 0` is a neat way to ask "is n even?". You'll use exactly that next.
